qa_pairs.question: How can a virtual machine (VM) hosted on the Cloud access filesystems from the national HPC clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: VMs hosted on our Cloud do not have direct access to the filesystems on the national HPC clusters. To access files stored on /project from a VM, users should use SSHFS.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of SSHFS in the context of virtual machines (VMs) and /project filesystems?
qa_pairs.answer: SSHFS enables mounting your /project folder inside your VM. These SSHFS mounts appear like regular folders in your VM and can be accessed via standard Linux commands.

qa_pairs.question: What are the essential security requirements for mounting a /project filesystem inside a virtual machine (VM) using SSHFS?
qa_pairs.answer: To avoid security issues when mounting /project inside your VM, you must not store your password in plain text on the VM, create and only use an SSH key specifically for SSHFS (not your login key), and keep your VM up to date with only required and secured ports open.
